input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:

seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:
`.testsettings` files are **not supported** by modern `dotnet test` — that's why your settings are ignored. You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's what your `.testsettings` configures and how to migrate each part:

**1. Create a `.runsettings` file** replacing `local.testsettings`:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <!-- Replaces <Properties><Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" /> -->
  <MSTest>
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <!-- Replaces <AgentRule><DataCollectors> code coverage collector -->
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests?\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has an MSTest v3 project with a `.testsettings` file. The `.testsettings` format is legacy and not supported by modern MSTest / dotnet test. They need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file.

Let me invoke the migrate-mstest-v1v2-to-v3 skill since it covers replacing `.testsettings` with `.runsettings`.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <!-- Replaces <Properties><Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" /> -->
  <MSTest>
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <!-- Replaces <AgentRule><DataCollectors> code coverage collector -->
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests?\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content: **2. Wire it up in your `.csproj`** so it's used automatically:

Here's a summary of the migration:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout></MSTest>` |
| `<AssemblyResolution>` / deployment | `<MSTest><DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled></MSTest>` (deployment is rarely needed now) |
| `<DataCollector>` for Code Coverage | `<DataCollectionRunSettings><DataCollectors>` with updated URI (`2.0`) |

**Key points:**
- The old `local.testsettings` file can be **deleted** — it has no effect on `dotnet test`.
- You can also pass the settings explicitly: `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`
- For code coverage collection, run: `dotnet test --collect "Code Coverage"`
